title: Week of Feb 24 - Mar 1, 2026
summary: Instrument watchlist with live IB quotes, custom charting system, P&L history tab, and commission capture fix.

features.title: Instrument Watchlist
features.summary: Create named watchlists with live IB price data for stocks, indices, and futures on the Charts page
features.description:
A new Watchlist tab on the Charts page lets you create and manage named lists of instruments with live price data from Interactive Brokers.

Key features:
- Create multiple named watchlists (e.g., "Core Positions", "Futures Watch")
- Add stocks, indices, and futures with SEC EDGAR ticker autocomplete
- Live price table: last price, change, change %, volume, bid/ask — all from IB
- 8-second auto-refresh with smart polling (pauses when tab is hidden)
- Green/red color coding for positive/negative changes
- ColumnChooser for user-controlled column visibility
- Full comfort mode support via d-table fluid scaling
- Rename, delete, and switch between watchlists
- Smart futures support: auto-detects exchange from symbol (no manual dropdown), accepts both bare roots (ES) and full contracts (SIK6, CLJ6)
- Futures contract month display: shows embedded month code for specific contracts (SIK6 → K6) or derived front month for bare roots (ES → H6)
- Reorder tickers with up/down arrows on each row — order persists across sessions
- Adaptive number formatting for mobile: prices use fewer decimals at higher magnitudes

The watchlist data is stored per-user in the database. Quotes are fetched in batch through the existing IB relay infrastructure — same pipeline used by the trading tools.

features.title: Custom Charting System
features.summary: TradingView-quality candlestick charts with BMC signal overlays and trade entry/exit markers in a drag-and-drop widget grid
features.description:
A new Charts tab in M&A Options brings professional-grade candlestick charts powered by lightweight-charts v5 and Polygon.io market data.

Key features:
- Full OHLCV candlestick + volume histogram charts for any ticker
- Timeframe selector: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 1D
- BMC signal overlay: probability markers (arrows with percentages) and signal histogram showing call/put direction with color intensity
- Trade markers: entry arrows (green BUY) and exit arrows (colored by P&L) with trailing stop, stop loss, and profit target labels
- Drag-and-drop widget grid with signal panel and positions panel
- Layout persistence via localStorage
- Independent polling: 30s bars, 5s signals, 10s execution (all pause when tab is hidden)

Architecturally, charts are completely isolated from the trading engine. They read from Polygon.io via a server-side API proxy and overlay signals from the existing BMC/execution polling endpoints. If charts crash, trading is unaffected.

features.title: P&L History Tab
features.summary: Historical algo trade performance with grouped aggregations, position detail, and expandable fill rows
features.description:
A new P&L History tab on the IB Trading Tools page shows historical algorithmic trade performance from PostgreSQL.

Key features:
- Summary totals bar: total trades, win rate, gross P&L, commission, and net P&L
- Group-by selector: toggle between date, symbol, or model version aggregation
- Clickable summary rows filter the positions table to that group
- Position detail table with ColumnChooser (14 columns, user-configurable)
- Expandable rows: click any position to see individual fills with time, level, qty, price, P&L %, commission, and exchange
- Date range and status filters (closed/active/all)
- Green/red P&L coloring, exit reason badges (risk/expired/manual), and level badges (entry/trailing/expired)
- Full comfort mode support via d-table-wrap fluid scaling

All data comes from the deployed P&L history API (v1.16.0) — zero backend changes needed.

features.title: Commission & P&L Capture Fix
features.summary: Fixed two bugs preventing commission and realized P&L from being stored in the trade history pipeline
features.description:
Two bugs in the execution engine prevented commission data from being captured and stored:

1. The fill persistence code read execId from IB's orderStatus callback, which never includes it — only the execDetails callback carries execId. Every fill was stored with an empty exec_id, so the deferred commission lookup never matched.

2. IB's commissionReport callback was routed through the exec_details_listeners with order_id=0. The engine's guard clause dropped these events since order_id 0 was never in the strategy map.

The fix adds a three-path commission capture system:
- Event-driven: commission events are now classified as a distinct event type and routed directly via an exec_id → position map
- execDetails enrichment: when execDetails arrives, the engine retroactively sets exec_id on the fill and triggers a deferred commission poll
- Fallback polling: the existing deferred poll now works correctly with real exec_ids (timeout increased from 2s to 5s)

This means all future trades will have commission costs and IB-reported realized P&L captured in the P&L History tab. Agent v1.17.1.

features.title: Futures Charts via IB
features.summary: Candlestick charts now support futures instruments using IB historical data
features.description:
Charts automatically detect futures tickers (ES, NQ, CL, GC, etc.) and fetch OHLCV bar data from Interactive Brokers instead of Polygon. Supports all standard timeframes (1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 1D) with the same 30-second auto-refresh.

Futures symbols are auto-detected from a known list including equity indices (ES, NQ, YM, RTY), energy (CL, NG), metals (GC, SI), treasuries (ZB, ZN), and grains (ZC, ZS, ZW). Both bare root symbols (ES → continuous front-month via CONTFUT) and full contract tickers (ESH6 → March 2026 specific contract) are supported. Exchange routing uses the same mapping as the trading engine. An amber "IB" badge appears next to the ticker when chart data comes from Interactive Brokers. Agent v1.19.6.

features.title: Watchlist Quote Reliability Fix
features.summary: Fixed batch quote fetching that was overwhelming the IB agent and causing complete data loss
features.description:
The watchlist's batch quote fetcher was sending all 18 ticker requests concurrently to the IB agent, which processes them sequentially. This caused WebSocket disconnections and prevented any data from reaching the UI.

Three fixes:
1. Chunked batch requests (max 4 concurrent) to prevent agent overload
2. Per-request 10-second timeout via AbortSignal.timeout to bound total fetch time
3. Overlap guard in the polling hook to prevent cascading fetches

Also fixed monthly commodity futures front-month derivation — CL/NG/GC/SI contracts expire weeks before the delivery month, so the agent now uses the next month for monthly symbols. Silver (SI) contracts are disambiguated with the standard 5000oz multiplier. Agent v1.19.6.

features.title: Stock Quote Change/Volume Fix
features.summary: Fixed stock instruments showing no change, change%, or volume data in the watchlist
features.description:
Stock instruments in the watchlist showed last price but Change, Change%, and Volume columns were blank. The root cause: the Polygon snapshot API returns previous close (prevDay.c) and today's volume (day.v) but the quote fetcher wasn't extracting them. Without the previous close, the frontend couldn't compute change or change%.

Fixed by extracting close and volume from the Polygon snapshot response and passing them through the relay pipeline. Futures were unaffected since they use the IB data path which already included close and volume.

Note: outside market hours (evenings/weekends), stock prices reflect the last trade from the previous session — this is expected since equity markets are closed. Futures show live Sunday–Friday prices via CME Globex.

features.title: Futures Chart Reliability Fix
features.summary: Fixed futures chart 500 errors by consolidating duplicated detection code into shared utilities
features.description:
Futures charts (ESH6, NQM26, etc.) were failing with 'Failed to fetch IB bars: 500' because the chart component had its own duplicated futures detection code that diverged from the shared module the watchlist uses successfully.

Consolidated ~60 lines of duplicated futures symbols, exchange mapping, month codes, and contract parsing from ChartWidgetInstance into the shared futures-utils.ts module. Added a new getContractMonth() helper that converts parsed contract data into the YYYYMM format the IB bars API expects. Both charts and watchlist now use identical futures resolution logic.

features.title: Mobile Chart Ticker Editing
features.summary: Fixed inability to tap the ticker input or timeframe buttons on mobile devices
features.description:
On mobile devices, the ticker input field and timeframe buttons (1m/5m/15m/1h/1D) in chart widget headers were completely unresponsive to touch. The entire header div was marked as a drag-handle for react-grid-layout, which intercepted all touch events — including taps on interactive controls.

Fixed by splitting the header: only the grip icon and title text are now inside the drag-handle zone. The ticker input, IB badge, timeframe buttons, and close button sit outside it, so touch events reach them normally. Desktop dragging still works by grabbing the grip icon or title. Also widened the ticker input (56px → 64px) and increased its font size for easier mobile tapping.

features.title: Intraday Chart Timestamp Fix
features.summary: Fixed sub-1-hour futures charts showing daily bars instead of intraday candles
features.description:
Sub-hourly futures charts (1m, 5m, 15m) were displaying only one giant bar per day instead of hundreds of intraday candles. The x-axis showed daily date labels instead of intraday times.

Root cause: IB's reqHistoricalData was called with formatDate=1, which returns intraday bar timestamps as 'YYYYMMDD  HH:MM:SS' strings. The scanner's bar handler tried int(bar.date), which threw ValueError on the space-separated format. The except block stripped the time component, collapsing all bars on the same day to a midnight timestamp. The charting library then deduplicated by timestamp, showing one bar per day.

Fixed by switching formatDate from 1 to 2, which makes IB return epoch seconds directly for intraday bars (and YYYYMMDD for daily). The existing int() parse handles both correctly. Agent v1.19.9.

features.title: OHLCV Crosshair Legend
features.summary: Charts now display O/H/L/C values and volume when hovering over any bar
features.description:
Hovering the crosshair over a candlestick bar now shows a TradingView-style OHLCV legend in the top-left corner of the chart. Values are colored green for bullish bars and red for bearish bars, with volume shown in compact format (K/M suffixes). The legend disappears when the crosshair moves off the chart area.

The legend uses a semi-transparent dark background (bg-gray-950/80) for readability against any chart content, with grouped O/H/L/C/V labels and adequate spacing. Works on both stock and futures charts across all timeframes.

features.title: Timeframe Switch Data Fix
features.summary: Fixed charts showing stale candles from the previous timeframe when switching between 1m/5m/15m/1h/1D
features.description:
Switching timeframes (e.g., 5m → 15m) would sometimes show the old timeframe's candles until new data loaded. The chart wasn't being cleared when the data source reset to empty during the transition.

Fixed by explicitly clearing the chart's candlestick and volume data when bars are empty, ensuring a clean slate before the new timeframe's data arrives. Also added bar count to the widget title (e.g., 'SPY — 5m (390)') as a quick verification that the correct number of bars loaded for each timeframe.

features.title: Chart Widget Error Isolation
features.summary: Individual chart widgets now recover gracefully from errors instead of crashing the entire page
features.description:
Added React error boundaries around each chart widget so that if one widget encounters bad data or a rendering error (e.g., from IB returning duplicate timestamps for futures bars), only that widget shows an error state with a Retry button — the rest of the page remains functional.

Also added duplicate-timestamp deduplication for bar data from IB (which can return overlapping bars during polling), and defensive try-catch wrappers around all chart library data-setting operations. Previously, an error in any single widget would crash the entire charts page with no recovery path.

features.title: Built-in Preset Protection
features.summary: Built-in chart presets (Single Chart, Quad View) are no longer silently overwritten when you customize tickers
features.description:
Previously, modifying tickers in a built-in preset (e.g., changing Quad View's default SPY tickers to ES-NQ-GC-SI) would auto-save over the built-in preset. Next time you loaded Quad View for a fresh layout, the customized version would load instead.

Built-in presets are now read-only. Ticker changes on built-in presets are not auto-saved. To keep a customized layout, use Save As to create a named copy. Also fixed the /charts URL defaulting to the Watchlist tab instead of Charts.

features.title: Persistent Daily Change Overlay
features.summary: Charts now show a persistent price change and percentage overlay at the top-right, visible without hovering
features.description:
Each chart widget now displays a persistent daily change overlay at the top-right corner showing the price change and percentage (e.g., '+2.45 (+0.52%)' in green or '-1.30 (-0.28%)' in red). Unlike the OHLCV crosshair legend (which only appears on hover), this overlay is always visible.

The calculation adapts to the timeframe:
- Intraday (1m/5m/15m/1h): shows session change from the first bar's open to the last bar's close
- Daily (1D): shows the most recent day's change vs the previous day's close

The overlay uses the same semi-transparent dark background as the crosshair legend for visual consistency, positioned at top-right to avoid overlapping with the top-left OHLCV legend. Works on both stock and futures charts across all timeframes.
